data.narrative:
Ursa Space Systems is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised approximately $67M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies: Sierra Space ($8.0B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), and Skyryse ($1.1B). A broader comparables universe includes 5 public Space & Aerospace equities (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across the three private comparables is $1.5B, with the peer range spanning $1.1B to $8.0B. This wide dispersion reflects material variation in company scale and business model maturity within the Series C cohort. Public company anchoring was not applied, as Ursa's revenue is not disclosed—a common constraint in private company valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $1.7B (high), centered on $1.5B. This range reflects the peer-median result with modest adjustment for comparable dispersal. Key uncertainties include Ursa's revenue scale relative to peers and the outsized valuation of Sierra Space ($8.0B), which may reflect differentiated technology or downstream integration. The single-method derivation represents a material constraint on conviction.
